speaker: FALSTAFF
text: The Windsor bell hath struck twelve. The

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: minute draws on. Now, the hot-blooded gods assist

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: me! Remember, Jove, thou wast a bull for thy

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Europa; love set on thy horns. O powerful love,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: that in some respects makes a beast a man, in

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: some other a man a beast! You were also, Jupiter,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: a swan for the love of Leda. O omnipotent love,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: how near the god drew to the complexion of a

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: goose! A fault done first in the form of a beast; O

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Jove, a beastly fault! And then another fault in the

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: semblance of a fowl; think on ’t, Jove, a foul fault.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: When gods have hot backs, what shall poor men

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: do? For me, I am here a Windsor stag, and the fattest,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I think, i’ th’ forest. Send me a cool rut-time,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Jove, or who can blame me to piss my tallow?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Who comes here? My doe?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: Sir John? Art thou there, my deer, my

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: male deer?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: My doe with the black scut! Let the sky rain

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: potatoes, let it thunder to the tune of “Greensleeves,”

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: hail kissing-comfits, and snow eryngoes; let there

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: come a tempest of provocation, I will shelter me

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: here.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: Mistress Page is come with me,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: sweetheart.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Divide me like a bribed buck, each a haunch.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I will keep my sides to myself, my shoulders for

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: the fellow of this walk, and my horns I bequeath

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: your husbands. Am I a woodman, ha? Speak I like

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Herne the Hunter? Why, now is Cupid a child of

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: conscience; he makes restitution. As I am a true

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: spirit, welcome.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Alas, what noise?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: Heaven forgive our sins!

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: What should this be?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
               
              and
               
              MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Away, away.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I think the devil will not have me damned,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: lest the oil that’s in me should set hell on fire. He

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: would never else cross me thus.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Fairies black, gray, green, and white,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: You moonshine revelers and shades of night,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: You orphan heirs of fixèd destiny,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Attend your office and your quality.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Crier Hobgoblin, make the fairy oyes.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Elves, list your names. Silence, you airy toys!—

speaker: PISTOL
text: Cricket, to Windsor chimneys shalt thou leap,

speaker: PISTOL
text: Where fires thou find’st unraked and hearths unswept.

speaker: PISTOL
text: There pinch the maids as blue as bilberry.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Our radiant queen hates sluts and sluttery.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: They are fairies. He that speaks to them shall die.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I’ll wink and couch. No man their works must eye.

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Where’s Bead? Go you, and where you find a maid

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: That ere she sleep has thrice her prayers said,

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Raise up the organs of her fantasy;

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Sleep she as sound as careless infancy.

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: But those as sleep and think not on their sins,

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Pinch them, arms, legs, backs, shoulders, sides, and shins.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: About, about!

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Search Windsor Castle, elves, within and out.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Strew good luck, aufs, on every sacred room,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: That it may stand till the perpetual doom

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: In state as wholesome as in state ’tis fit,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Worthy the owner, and the owner it.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: The several chairs of order look you scour

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: With juice of balm and every precious flower.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Each fair installment, coat, and sev’ral crest

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: With loyal blazon evermore be blest!

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: And nightly, meadow fairies, look you sing,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Like to the Garter’s compass, in a ring.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Th’ expressure that it bears, green let it be,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: More fertile-fresh than all the field to see;

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: And Honi soit qui mal y pense write

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: In em’rald tufts, flowers purple, blue, and white,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Like sapphire, pearl, and rich embroidery,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Buckled below fair knighthood’s bending knee.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Fairies use flowers for their charactery.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Away, disperse! But till ’tis one o’clock,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Our dance of custom round about the oak

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Of Herne the Hunter let us not forget.

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Pray you, lock hand in hand. Yourselves in order set;

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: And twenty glowworms shall our lanterns be,

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: To guide our measure round about the tree.

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: But stay! I smell a man of Middle Earth.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Heavens defend me from that Welsh

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: fairy, lest he transform me to a piece of cheese.

speaker: PISTOL
text: Vile worm, thou wast o’erlooked even in thy birth.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: With trial-fire touch me his finger-end.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: If he be chaste, the flame will back descend

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: And turn him to no pain. But if he start,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: It is the flesh of a corrupted heart.

speaker: PISTOL
text: A trial, come!

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Come, will this wood take fire?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: O, O, O!

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: Corrupt, corrupt, and tainted in desire!

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: About him, fairies. Sing a scornful rhyme,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              QUICKLY
text: And, as you trip, still pinch him to your time.

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Fie on sinful fantasy!

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Fie on lust and luxury!

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Lust is but a bloody fire

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Kindled with unchaste desire,

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Fed in heart whose flames aspire

speaker: FAIRIES
text: As thoughts do blow them higher and higher.

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Pinch him, fairies, mutually;

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Pinch him for his villainy.

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Pinch him and burn him and turn him about,

speaker: FAIRIES
text: Till candles and starlight and moonshine be out.

speaker: PAGE
text: Nay, do not fly. I think we have watched you now.

speaker: PAGE
text: Will none but Herne the Hunter serve your turn?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: I pray you, come, hold up the jest no higher.—

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Now, good Sir John, how like you Windsor wives?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: See you these, husband? Do not these fair yokes

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Become the forest better than the town?

speaker: FORD
text: Now, sir, who’s a cuckold now?

speaker: FORD
text: Master Brook, Falstaff’s a knave, a cuckoldly

speaker: FORD
text: knave. Here are his horns, Master Brook. And,

speaker: FORD
text: Master Brook, he hath enjoyed nothing of Ford’s

speaker: FORD
text: but his buck-basket, his cudgel, and twenty

speaker: FORD
text: pounds of money, which must be paid to Master

speaker: FORD
text: Brook. His horses are arrested for it, Master

speaker: FORD
text: Brook.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: Sir John, we have had ill luck. We

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: could never meet. I will never take you for my love

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              FORD
text: again, but I will always count you my deer.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I do begin to perceive that I am made an ass.

speaker: FORD
text: Ay, and an ox too. Both the proofs are extant.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: And these are not fairies. I was three or four

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: times in the thought they were not fairies; and yet

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: the guiltiness of my mind, the sudden surprise of

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: my powers, drove the grossness of the foppery into

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: a received belief, in despite of the teeth of all

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: rhyme and reason, that they were fairies. See now

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: how wit may be made a Jack-a-Lent when ’tis upon

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: ill employment.

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Sir John Falstaff, serve Got and leave your

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: desires, and fairies will not pinse you.

speaker: FORD
text: Well said, Fairy Hugh.

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: And leave you your jealousies too, I pray

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: you.

speaker: FORD
text: I will never mistrust my wife again till thou art

speaker: FORD
text: able to woo her in good English.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Have I laid my brain in the sun and dried it,

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: that it wants matter to prevent so gross o’erreaching

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: as this? Am I ridden with a Welsh goat too?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Shall I have a coxcomb of frieze? ’Tis time I were

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: choked with a piece of toasted cheese.

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: Seese is not good to give putter. Your belly is

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: all putter.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: “Seese” and “putter”? Have I lived to stand at

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: the taunt of one that makes fritters of English?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: This is enough to be the decay of lust and late

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: walking through the realm.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Why, Sir John, do you think though we

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: would have thrust virtue out of our hearts by the

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: head and shoulders, and have given ourselves

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: without scruple to hell, that ever the devil could

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: have made you our delight?

speaker: FORD
text: What, a hodge-pudding? A bag of flax?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: A puffed man?

speaker: PAGE
text: Old, cold, withered, and of intolerable entrails?

speaker: FORD
text: And one that is as slanderous as Satan?

speaker: PAGE
text: And as poor as Job?

speaker: FORD
text: And as wicked as his wife?

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: And given to fornications, and to taverns,

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: and sack, and wine, and metheglins, and to drinkings

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: and swearings and starings, pribbles and

speaker:
SIR
               
              HUGH
text: prabbles?

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Well, I am your theme. You have the start of

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: me. I am dejected. I am not able to answer the

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: Welsh flannel. Ignorance itself is a plummet o’er

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: me. Use me as you will.

speaker: FORD
text: Marry, sir, we’ll bring you to Windsor to one

speaker: FORD
text: Master Brook, that you have cozened of money,

speaker: FORD
text: to whom you should have been a pander. Over and

speaker: FORD
text: above that you have suffered, I think to repay that

speaker: FORD
text: money will be a biting affliction.

speaker: PAGE
text: Yet be cheerful, knight. Thou shalt eat a posset

speaker: PAGE
text: tonight at my house, where I will desire thee to

speaker: PAGE
text: laugh at my wife, that now laughs at thee. Tell her

speaker: PAGE
text: Master Slender hath married her daughter.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Doctors doubt that. If Anne

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Page be my daughter, she is, by this, Doctor Caius’

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: wife.

speaker: SLENDER
text: Whoa, ho, ho, Father Page!

speaker: PAGE
text: Son, how now! How now, son! Have you

speaker: PAGE
text: dispatched?

speaker: SLENDER
text: “Dispatched”? I’ll make the best in Gloucestershire

speaker: SLENDER
text: know on ’t. Would I were hanged, la, else!

speaker: PAGE
text: Of what, son?

speaker: SLENDER
text: I came yonder at Eton to marry Mistress

speaker: SLENDER
text: Anne Page, and she’s a great lubberly boy. If it had

speaker: SLENDER
text: not been i’ th’ church, I would have swinged him,

speaker: SLENDER
text: or he should have swinged me. If I did not think it

speaker: SLENDER
text: had been Anne Page, would I might never stir! And

speaker: SLENDER
text: ’tis a post-master’s boy.

speaker: PAGE
text: Upon my life, then, you took the wrong—

speaker: SLENDER
text: What need you tell me that? I think so, when

speaker: SLENDER
text: I took a boy for a girl. If I had been married to him,

speaker: SLENDER
text: for all he was in woman’s apparel, I would not

speaker: SLENDER
text: have had him.

speaker: PAGE
text: Why, this is your own folly. Did not I tell you

speaker: PAGE
text: how you should know my daughter by her

speaker: PAGE
text: garments?

speaker: SLENDER
text: I went to her in white, and cried “mum,”

speaker: SLENDER
text: and she cried “budget,” as Anne and I had appointed,

speaker: SLENDER
text: and yet it was not Anne, but a post-master’s

speaker: SLENDER
text: boy.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Good George, be not angry. I knew of

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: your purpose, turned my daughter into green,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: and indeed she is now with the doctor at the deanery,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: and there married.

speaker:
DOCTOR
               
              CAIUS
text: Vere is Mistress Page? By gar, I am cozened!

speaker:
DOCTOR
               
              CAIUS
text: I ha’ married un garçon, a boy; un paysan, by

speaker:
DOCTOR
               
              CAIUS
text: gar, a boy. It is not Anne Page. By gar, I am

speaker:
DOCTOR
               
              CAIUS
text: cozened.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Why? Did you take her in green?

speaker:
DOCTOR
               
              CAIUS
text: Ay, be gar, and ’tis a boy. Be gar, I’ll raise

speaker:
DOCTOR
               
              CAIUS
text: all Windsor.

speaker: FORD
text: This is strange. Who hath got the right Anne?

speaker: PAGE
text: My heart misgives me. Here comes Master Fenton.—

speaker: PAGE
text: How now, Master Fenton!

speaker: ANNE
text: Pardon, good father. Good my mother, pardon.

speaker: PAGE
text: Now, mistress, how chance you went not with

speaker: PAGE
text: Master Slender?

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Why went you not with Master Doctor, maid?

speaker: FENTON
text: You do amaze her. Hear the truth of it.

speaker: FENTON
text: You would have married her most shamefully,

speaker: FENTON
text: Where there was no proportion held in love.

speaker: FENTON
text: The truth is, she and I, long since contracted,

speaker: FENTON
text: Are now so sure that nothing can dissolve us.

speaker: FENTON
text: Th’ offense is holy that she hath committed,

speaker: FENTON
text: And this deceit loses the name of craft,

speaker: FENTON
text: Of disobedience, or unduteous title,

speaker: FENTON
text: Since therein she doth evitate and shun

speaker: FENTON
text: A thousand irreligious cursèd hours

speaker: FENTON
text: Which forcèd marriage would have brought upon her.

speaker: FORD
text: Stand not amazed. Here is no remedy.

speaker: FORD
text: In love the heavens themselves do guide the state.

speaker: FORD
text: Money buys lands, and wives are sold by fate.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: I am glad, though you have ta’en a special

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: stand to strike at me, that your arrow hath

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: glanced.

speaker: PAGE
text: Well, what remedy? Fenton, heaven give thee joy.

speaker: PAGE
text: What cannot be eschewed must be embraced.

speaker: FALSTAFF
text: When night-dogs run, all sorts of deer are chased.

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Well, I will muse no further.—Master Fenton,

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Heaven give you many, many merry days.—

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Good husband, let us every one go home

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: And laugh this sport o’er by a country fire—

speaker:
MISTRESS
               
              PAGE
text: Sir John and all.

speaker: FORD
text: Let it be so, Sir John.

speaker: FORD
text: To Master Brook you yet shall hold your word,

speaker: FORD
text: For he tonight shall lie with Mistress Ford.
